name: Dark Chocolate and Pumpkin Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 8 ounces semisweet chocolate, broken into 1/2-oz pieces
2 cups semisweet chocolate, broken into 1/2-oz pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 325°F (160°C). Toast pecan pieces on a baking sheet in the preheated oven for 6-8 minutes. Remove from the oven and allow to cool to room temperature.
2. Lower the oven temperature to 300°F (150°C).
3. In a food processor fitted with a metal blade, chop cookies and pecans into crumbs. Set aside until needed.
4. Coat the bottom of a 9-by-3-inch springform pan with 1 tsp melted butter. Press 1 cup of crumbs evenly onto the buttered bottom of the pan. Set aside.
5. For chocolate cheesecake:
- Heat 1 inch of water in the bottom half of a double boiler over medium-high heat.
- Place semisweet chocolate and coffee in the top half of the double boiler. Cover tightly with plastic wrap.
- Allow to heat for 6-8 minutes, then remove from heat and stir until smooth.
- In a bowl fitted with a paddle attachment on an electric mixer, beat softened cream cheese, sugar, and salt for 1 minute on low speed, then 2 minutes on medium speed.
- Scrape the sides of the bowl, then beat on medium for 2 more minutes and high for 3 minutes. Scrape the bowl again.
- Add eggs one at a time, beating until each is incorporated before adding the next. Beat for an additional minute after all eggs are added.
- Stir in vanilla extract for 15 seconds, then continue beating for another 2 minutes.
- Add melted chocolate mixture and beat on medium for 30 seconds.
- Pour the cheesecake batter into the prepared pan and refrigerate until set (about 1 hour).
6. For pumpkin cheesecake:
- Coat a 9-by-11-inch cake pan with 1 tsp melted butter and line with parchment paper. Add remaining butter to the parchment.
- In a bowl fitted with a paddle attachment on an electric mixer, beat softened cream cheese, sugar, flour, and salt until smooth (about 5 minutes).
- Add eggs one at a time, beating until each is incorporated before adding the next. Stir in vanilla extract for 15 seconds.
- Add pumpkin puree, spices, and beat for another 30 seconds.
- Pour batter into prepared pan and place both pans on the center rack of the oven preheated to 300°F (150°C).
- Bake for 1 hour and 10 minutes, or until internal temperature reaches 160°F (70°C). Turn off the oven and let cheesecakes cool in the oven for an additional 20 minutes.
7. Cool chocolate cheesecake in the refrigerator for 1 hour. Refrigerate pumpkin cheesecake at room temperature.
8. Pour Semisweet Chocolate Ganache over chilled chocolate cheesecake, spreading evenly. Invert pumpkin cheesecake onto ganache layer and press gently to set.
9. Wrap springform pan with plastic wrap and refrigerate until set (at least 3 hours).
10. Release cheesecake from the pan, remove plastic wrap, and wrap damp towel around sides of the pan for about 1 minute. Carefully release and remove springform pan.
11. Smooth cake sides with ganache using a cake spatula, then spread remaining ganache over top. Press remaining crumbs into cake sides.
12. Refrigerate until ready to serve. Cut and serve on plates.
Note: Serve immediately after cutting. Enjoy!
